description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves create a striped pattern, giving the block a visually distinct, textured appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a dark coloration that contrasts with the lighter, slightly mottled surface of the grooved top. The block appears dense and robust, likely made from a heavy stone material such as granite or basalt. Its proportions suggest it is designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly to control pedestrian or vehicular movement. The overall finish is matte with a rough texture, indicating durability and resistance to weathering.
description: A rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and smooth, solid sides, featuring a dark, mottled stone appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the horizontal grooves running across the top surface, with the block's solid, dark face visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the parallel grooves and the block's rectangular profile.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same grooved pattern and solid base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
